body.title: Rebuild skill_telemetry.py — upstream sprint never committed
body.result: skill_telemetry.py created in TheIgors lab/claudecode/ + 10 tests; all functions present (TheIgors 7876a619)
body.description:
**What happened:** T-skill-telemetry-schema, T-skill-telemetry-logger, T-skill-telemetry-rollup all marked closed but skill_telemetry.py was never committed to the repo. Discovered by T-consequence-skill-telemetry consequence check.

**What to build:** lab/claudecode/skill_telemetry.py with:
- TypedDicts: SkillContract, ViolationRecord, OutcomeRecord
- append_violation(skill, flag, context) → appends to IGOR_HOME/claudecode/violation_log.jsonl
- append_outcome(skill, metric, value) → appends to IGOR_HOME/claudecode/outcome_log.jsonl
- top_violations(n=5, days=30) → list[(skill, flag, count)] sorted desc
- skill_outcome_trend(skill, metric, days=30) → slope/direction
- monthly_rollup() → dict summarizing last 30 days

**Log location:** IGOR_HOME/claudecode/violation_log.jsonl, outcome_log.jsonl (IGOR_HOME defaults to ~/.TheIgors)

context-load/run already imports it correctly at `from lab.claudecode.skill_telemetry import top_violations` — just needs the file to exist.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/skill_telemetry.py (create), tests/test_skill_telemetry.py (create)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** File creation only; no changes to context-load/run or SKILL.md
**Test plan:** Test append_violation round-trip, top_violations with fixture log, graceful empty-log handling
